titleStmt.title: SII 16.206: original edition by H. K. Narasimhaswami and G. S. Gai
No. 206. (A.R. No. 372 of 1920.) NĀGALĀPURAM, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a stone lying in front of the Āñjanēya shrine.
This is dated Śaka 1777, apparently wrong for Śaka 1477, Rākshasa, Āśvīja, śu. 2 corresponding to 1555 A.D., September 17 (Tuesday).

This damaged record seems to register a gift of the village Ēṭivīḍu alias Nāgalāpuram for provision of rice, green-gram and oil for the lamp for the Kārttika festivals in the temple of Śiva (name not clear) for the merit of the Seṭṭi-paṭṭasvāmins, the gaṇāchāryas, etc., the disciples of Bhikshāvṛitti-mahārāya.
The gift village is stated to be situated in Jagatāpigutti-sīma ruled over by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Śrīraṅgarājayyadēva-mahārāju.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . svastiśrī jayābhyudaya śāli
vāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu 1477 Agunēṭi
rākṣasa saṁvatsara Āśvīja śu 2 lu śrīmanmahā
rājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa śrīsa
dāsivadēvamahārāyalu pṛdhvirājyaṁ cēyu
cuṁnnu śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara śrīraṁgarājayya dēva
mahārājugārka pāliṁcina jagatāpiguttisīmalōni Ēṭivī
ṭi grāmānaku pratināmamayina nāgalāpuraṁ sa¨rvamā
nyaṁ Agrahāraṁ śrīmatkailāsavāsulayi
na triśūla ḍamaru gada khaḍga liṁgānaku
ḍa vīrapurāṁtulu baṁṭugā sēviṁpaṁga malahari
gāru kā¨rttika mahātiranālakuṁnu di 1 ki biyaṁnū 1
panneṁḍu pesalu tu 1 dīpārādhanaku nūne ma 8 ki
cēsēṭaṭugā sahastamastakayōgaĀcārapratipālanaku
Āgama cāra bhikṣāvrittimahārāya divyaśrīpādārā
dhakulaku puṁṇyaṁmugānu dēśādhīśa
samasta seṭṭipaṭṭasvāmulaku puṁṇyaṁgānu
ratnasiṁhāsanuṁ
śrīviprānuliṁggasamaṁtulai
na mahaṁtulakuṁnnu tripurāṁtalaku vīrapurāṁtulaku gaṇācā
rulaku puṁṇyamugānu dhāravōsi yistimi yī dha¨rmānaku yavva
ru tappinā doli Anumatini mīre drōhulu
naḍapenivāru śunakamāṁsaṁ surā
pānaṁ sēviṁcina pāpāna pōduru Anivrāyiṁccina dha¨rma śāsa
namu śrīśrīśrī .
Digital edition of SII 16.206 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
